title: Deploying a Digital Store on GitHub Pages: Everything You Need to Get Started
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: From zero to live crypto store in one afternoon. GitHub Pages hosting, Core Shop Engine, Web3 Wallet Connect, NOWPayments integration.

content.text: You're a digital creator in Ethiopia, and you've been struggling to find a way to sell your ebooks, courses, and software to your audience. You've tried using PayPal, but they won't let you open an account due to geographic restrictions. Stripe is no better, and you've lost count of the number of times you've been rejected from opening a merchant account. You're not alone. Millions of digital creators around the world face similar challenges. You need a solution that works, and works now. That's where this guide comes in – to help you deploy a digital store on GitHub Pages, using a payment and delivery pipeline that's been tested and proven to work in even the most restrictive environments. With the right tools and a clear plan, you can start selling your digital products to your audience in no time.
Why This Method Works When Everything Else Fails

The beauty of deploying a digital store on GitHub Pages lies in its simplicity and flexibility. Unlike traditional e-commerce platforms that require complicated setup and expensive payment processing fees, GitHub Pages allows you to host your store for free, and connect it to a payment pipeline that's designed to work in even the most restrictive environments. With this method, you can avoid the hassles of setting up a merchant account, dealing with KYC requirements, and navigating the complex world of payment processing. It's a game-changer for digital creators who have been left behind by the traditional e-commerce landscape.
What You Need Before You Start

Before you begin, you'll need a few basic tools and a clear plan. First, you'll need to create a GitHub account and set up a new repository for your store. Next, you'll need to install the Full Stack Bundle, a proven payment and delivery pipeline that's been tested and optimized for maximum performance. Finally, you'll need to configure your store settings, including your store name, description, and payment information. With these basics in place, you'll be ready to start building your store and connecting it to your payment pipeline.
Setting Up Your Payment and Delivery Pipeline

The payment and delivery pipeline is the heart of your digital store, and it's where the magic happens. With the Full Stack Bundle, you'll be able to connect your store to a payment processor that's designed to work in even the most restrictive environments. This means you'll be able to accept payments from your audience, deliver your digital products, and track your sales and revenue in real-time. It's a powerful and flexible system that's been tested and proven to work in even the most challenging environments.
The entire store stack — hosting, payments, delivery — has zero monthly costs after the one-time setup.
Going Live and Making Your First Sale

Once you've set up your payment and delivery pipeline, it's time to go live and make your first sale. This is the moment of truth, and it's where all your hard work and planning come together. With the Full Stack Bundle, you'll be able to track your sales and revenue in real-time, and you'll be able to make adjustments to your store settings and payment pipeline as needed. It's a powerful and flexible system that's designed to help you succeed, no matter what.
Scaling Once the System Is Running

Once you've made your first sale, it's time to start scaling your digital store and taking it to the next level. With the Full Stack Bundle, you'll be able to track your sales and revenue in real-time, and you'll be able to make adjustments to your store settings and payment pipeline as needed. You'll also be able to add new digital products, expand your marketing efforts, and connect with your audience in new and exciting ways. It's a powerful and flexible system that's designed to help you succeed, no matter what.
How do I build a digital store that accepts crypto payments?

No, you don't need to have technical expertise to deploy a digital store on GitHub Pages. The Full Stack Bundle is designed to be user-friendly and easy to use, even for those with limited technical experience. With a clear plan and a willingness to learn, you can deploy a digital store on GitHub Pages and start selling your digital products to your audience in no time.
Test a real mainnet transaction before you promote — finding a bug with your own money costs less than finding it with a buyer's.

With the right tools and a clear plan, you can start selling your digital products and achieving your goals in no time.
Your Action Checklist

Here's a checklist of the steps you need to take to deploy a digital store on GitHub Pages using the Full Stack Bundle: (1) Create a GitHub account and set up a new repository for your store, (2) install the Full Stack Bundle, (3) configure your store settings, including your store name, description, and payment information, (4) connect your store to a payment processor, (5) set up your payment and delivery pipeline, and (6) go live and make your first sale. With these steps in place, you'll be well on your way to deploying a digital store that's designed to help you succeed.
Four steps: deploy the engine, configure the wallet, test on mainnet, then and only then start promoting your store.
